title: Trump Winds Down the War He Started With Goals Unmet
summary: While the president says the agreement with Iran would open the Strait of Hormuz and provide economic relief, the country’s nuclear program is still a subject for negotiation.

title: Israel Counts the Ways That Netanyahu’s Iran Strategy Failed
summary: The U.S.-Iran framework agreement appears to omit some of the most important provisions that Israel wanted.

title: Frustrated by Courts, Trump Weighed Suspending a Constitutional Right
summary: Secret memos show that the White House debated last year, to a greater degree than previously known, whether to limit habeas corpus rights for undocumented immigrants.

title: What Is Habeas Corpus, and Why Are Trump Officials Talking About Suspending It?
summary: Administration officials have suggested suspending a legal principle that protects against unlawful detention, and struggled to accurately define it.

title: Gov. Gavin Newsom Says Trump Is Investigating Him and His Wife
summary: Aides to Gov. Gavin Newsom of California say several people associated with the couple have been contacted by federal agents in the past week. He criticized the move as politically motivated.

title: Algae Is Turning the Reflecting Pool Green. Again.
summary: Algal blooms have hit the site, between the Lincoln Memorial and the Washington Monument, after a $14.2 million repair project.

title: Lawmakers Warn Trump Officials Not to Pursue Arch Project Without Congress
summary: In a letter to Interior Secretary Doug Burgum and national parks officials, several Democrats and a Senate independent said that members of the administration could face fines and even criminal prosecution.

title: Kennedy Seeks to Expedite Appeal of Ruling That Blocked His Vaccine Policies
summary: The health secretary is trying to restart the work of a panel that advises the government on vaccines, after a judge froze its decisions and prevented it from meeting.

title: The Best TV Shows of 2026, So Far
summary: The first half of the year had few big, buzzy hits. But there were some nice surprises (“Widow’s Bay”) and returning favorites (“The Comeback”).

title: The Pain of Caring for a Parent Who Abused You
summary: The United States is reliant on unpaid family caregivers, and millions of adult children are caring for parents who didn’t really care for them.

title: A Bystander at a ‘No Kings’ March Was Killed. Did the Shooter Commit a Crime?
summary: A gunman believed he was defending the crowd, then killed an innocent protester. The legal case is complicated.

title: Millions of Women Are Left Out of Menopause’s Moment
summary: Women who can’t take hormone therapy because of breast cancer or other diseases say they are deeply frustrated.

title: SpaceX’s Stock Surges on First Full Day of Trading
summary: The company’s shares jumped on Monday, and executives announced that they had raised even more money in an already record-breaking initial offering.

title: Will SpaceX Fortunes Trickle Down to This Texas Town?
summary: Elon Musk built a huge complex outside the city of Bastrop, population 14,000. Its residents now wonder what the historic SpaceX initial public offering might mean for them.

title: If Democrats Want to Win the House, Their Battle May Start Here
summary: A bitterly fought Democratic primary in New York’s Hudson Valley will determine who takes on Representative Mike Lawler, one of the more vulnerable Republicans this cycle.

title: Alabama’s Senate Primary Runoff Again Tests Trump’s Hold on G.O.P.
summary: Representative Barry Moore, an early backer of the president during his first campaign, faces Jared Hudson, a former Navy SEAL tapping into the excitement for outsiders.

title: In Alabama, Opposition to Renewable Solar Energy Joins a Data Center Battle
summary: Tuesday’s runoff for a slot on the Alabama Public Service Commission has a familiar ring to it, with talk of data centers and electricity costs. But in a southern twist, solar power has joined the list of villains.

title: B-52 Bomber Crashes at Edwards Air Force Base in California
summary: The crash occurred shortly after takeoff this morning from Edwards Air Force Base in California. Emergency crews were responding to the site.

title: N.Y. Officials Reject Students’ Pleas to Move Exams for Knicks Parade
summary: The championship celebration starts at 10 a.m. Thursday, just as some of the state Regents tests begin. Students had organized petitions calling for the exams to be delayed.

title: How ‘The Furious’ Somersaults Through a Wild Action Scene
summary: The new movie is packed with showstopping action, but one standout sequence has our hero rolling over a pyramid of henchmen. Here’s what it took.

title: ‘The Furious’ Review: Action Spectacle Built on Body-Breaking Blows
summary: Xie Miao and Joe Taslim kick butt and take names in Kenji Tanigaki’s Asian action extravaganza.

title: ‘We Cannot Trust Any Iranian Promises’: 3 Opinion Writers Dissect the Iran Deal
summary: Was the Iran war worth it — and will the latest agreement even stick?

title: One City Might Have Just Cracked the Housing Crisis
summary: A large real estate development in the middle of Vancouver, British Columbia, shows how cities can build more housing.
